vision_analysis.primary_subject: interior drywall crack at door frame corner

visible_defects.defect: Diagonal drywall crack from top corner of interior door frame, ~18 inches

vision_analysis.context_notes: Door frame appears slightly out of square. Crack is hairline width.

input.transcript: Diagonal crack above the bedroom door, runs about a foot and a half. The door frame itself is a bit out of square — door doesn't latch cleanly. Could be settling, could be ongoing. Worth monitoring and re-checking in a year.

expected.rationale: SoP §3.3 lists sheetrock cracks + out-of-square door frames as possible foundation movement indicators. Inspector explicitly said monitor. Don't escalate to structural engineer unless severity warrants it.

source: InterNACHI SoP §3.3 — wood in contact with soil

vision_analysis.primary_subject: wood deck post at grade level

visible_defects.defect: Pressure-treated 4x4 deck post in direct contact with soil; visible rot at base

vision_analysis.context_notes: Probed with awl — wood is soft for the lower 4 inches.

input.transcript: Deck post is sitting right in the dirt, no concrete pier, no standoff. Base is rotted, probed soft. Needs to be lifted onto a proper post base.

expected.rationale: SoP explicitly requires reporting wood-soil contact. Rot already present = major repair, not monitor.

source: Foundation crack - distinguishing hairline vs. structural

vision_analysis.primary_subject: poured concrete foundation wall in basement

visible_defects.defect: Horizontal crack across foundation wall, ~1/4 inch wide at center

vision_analysis.context_notes: Crack runs horizontally for approx 6 ft. Slight inward bulge visible. No active water.

input.transcript: Basement wall, horizontal crack quarter-inch wide and running about six feet. Wall is bowing in. This is structural — recommend an engineer.

expected.rationale: Horizontal foundation crack with bowing = lateral soil pressure failure mode. This is the one foundation issue that escalates to safety_hazard. Engineer required.
